rulings.Case Title: PROMETHEUS REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al VS. WOODRUFF-SAWYER & COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 22, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT WOODRUFF-SAWYER & CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Triable issues of material fact exists as to whether the acts of the second broker are a superseding cause of Plaintiff¿s harm, whether plaintiff would have been able to get the coverage it claims it desired, and whether they could have been covered for the entirety of their claim. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the 1st cause of action for negligent failure to obtain the requested insurance coverage is denied. There are triable issues of materials facts regarding what Plaintiff requested and whether defendant¿s reliance on the questionnaire was sufficient. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the 3rd cause of action for breach of Contract is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to what the appropriate type of policy was warranted under the circumstances and if defendant selected the appropriate policy. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the 7th and 8th causes of action (fraud and misrepresentation) is granted. Based upon the undisputed evidence, defendant has shown that they did not misrepresent their experience or expertise when it was asserted. Moreover, even if it could be shown that those statements were factually inaccurate, they do not amount to intentional or negligent misrepresentation because plaintiff cannot show that they were inaccurate or that they were made with reckless disregard for the truth. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the 10th cause of action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether defendant was negligent in procuring the appropriate insurance for plaintiff and if this breached its fiduciary duty to Plaintiff.. rulings.Case Title: VICENTE CORTES VS. CURBSIDE TOO LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 22, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANT ANTOINE ALLIAUME'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD HIS FRAUD CLAIM SPECIFICALLY ENOUGH TO SATISFY PLEADING REQUIREMENTS FOR FRAUD CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF MUST INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING FACTS TO SUPPORT HIS CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD: -HOW DEFENDANT PROMISED TO GIVE PLAINTIFF OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN CURBSIDE. -WHEN DEFENDANT MADE THIS ALLEGED PROMISE. -WHERE DEFENDANT MADE THIS ALLEGED PROMISE. -TO WHOM DID DEFENDANT MAKE THIS PROMISE AND -BY WHAT MEANS THE REPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA DAHLIN VS. WILD SIDE WEST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 22, 2011, line 9, DEFENDANTS WILD SIDE WEST, BILLIE AS INDIVIDUAL, HAYES, DONNA ROBERTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO RECORD AN INSTRUMENT IN ACCORDANCE WITH CAPA. CC 987(h)(1). MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT'S DECLARATION DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY EXPLAIN HOW REMOVING PIPES, WINDOWS AND SIDING WOULD NOT CAUSE SUBSTANTIAL DEFACEMENT OF THE BUILDING. EVEN IF THE COURT ACCEPTED THIS EXPERT'S DECLARATION IN TOTO, THIS COURT FINDS THAT NO REASONABLE TRIER OF FACT COULD RULE IN PLAINTIFF'S FAVOR ON A VIOLATION OF CC 987. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PARON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC et al VS. ROTHSTEIN, KASS & COMPANY P.C.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rothstein, Kass & Company, Llp'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 22, 2011, line 17, Defendant Rothstein, Kass & Company, LLP'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Defendant¿s Demurrer to all causes of action brought by Paron Capital is sustained with leave to amend. In order to prevent these causes of action from being barred because it is in pari delicto, Paron must plead facts to show that Crombie¿s conduct was completely adverse to the company¿s interest and outside the scope of authority. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (negligent misrepresentation) as to Plaintiffs McConnon and Lyons is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that the report was intended to benefit them in their individual capacities. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (professional malpractice) as to Plaintiffs McConnon and Lyons is sustained without leave to amend. Only Paron was is contract with Defendant and thus, is the only party who can bring this claim. Bily v. Arthur Young (1992) 3 Cal.4th 370, 405. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (fraud) as to Plaintiffs McConnon and Lyons is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs must plead facts to show Defendant¿s intent to induce reliance on the representations and disclaimers in the Engagement Letter and Report. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: GERTRUDE BLAND PLATT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 22, 2011, line 16, RESPONDENTS CHIU, DAVID, PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS, EDWIN LEE, RICHARD JOHNS AMENDED DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. DEFENDANTS DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITIONERS HAVE NOT SHOWN QUO WARRANTO TO BE AN INADEQUATE ALTERNATIVE REMEDY. AS SUCH A WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS AN INAPPROPRIATE REMEDY PER CCP SECTION 1086. =(302/LMG)
